metadata.name: NBA Basketball (2020s)
metadata.description: The modern NBA from 2020 to today — championships, MVP awards, record-breakers, draft classes, and the biggest stars of the decade.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: The Golden State Warriors play their home games at which arena, located in San Francisco?
questions.tidbits: Chase Center opened in 2019, replacing Oracle Arena in Oakland as the Warriors' home.

questions.questionText: The Boston Celtics play their home games at which historic arena?
questions.tidbits: TD Garden has hosted the Celtics since 1995. The Celtics' 18 championships are the most in NBA history.

questions.questionText: The Milwaukee Bucks play in which conference — the Eastern or Western?
questions.tidbits: The Bucks play in the Central Division of the Eastern Conference alongside the Bulls, Pistons, Cavaliers, and Pacers.

questions.questionText: Which Texas city is home to the NBA's Spurs franchise, who drafted Victor Wembanyama first overall in 2023?
questions.tidbits: The Spurs are one of the most decorated franchises in NBA history, winning five championships between 1999 and 2014.

questions.questionText: The Denver Nuggets, 2023 NBA Champions, play their home games at which arena?
questions.tidbits: Ball Arena was previously called Pepsi Center and renamed when the Ball Corporation purchased naming rights in 2020.

questions.questionText: The Dallas Mavericks play at which arena in Dallas, Texas?
questions.tidbits: American Airlines Center has hosted both the Mavericks and the Dallas Stars NHL team since it opened in 2001.

questions.questionText: Which city's NBA team is nicknamed the Bucks, whose name refers to the white-tailed deer common in the region?
questions.tidbits: The Bucks were founded in 1968; their most famous alumni before 2021 include Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and Oscar Robertson.

questions.questionText: The Memphis Grizzlies originally relocated from which Canadian city before moving to Memphis in 2001?
questions.tidbits: The team was known as the Vancouver Grizzlies from 1995 to 2001 before relocating to Memphis, Tennessee.

questions.questionText: Which NBA team plays in Oklahoma City and was formerly the Seattle SuperSonics before relocating in 2008?
questions.tidbits: The Thunder's relocation from Seattle remains one of the most controversial franchise moves in NBA history.

questions.questionText: What new award did the NBA introduce in the 2023-24 season — a tournament played mid-season with its own bracket and finals?
questions.answerText: The NBA In-Season Tournament (Emirates NBA Cup)
questions.tidbits: The Los Angeles Lakers won the inaugural NBA In-Season Tournament, defeating the Indiana Pacers in the final in Las Vegas.

questions.questionText: Which Greek player, nicknamed 'The Greek Freak,' plays for the Milwaukee Bucks and won back-to-back MVPs in 2019 and 2020?
questions.tidbits: Giannis was born in Athens, Greece to Nigerian parents and was selected 15th overall in the 2013 draft by the Bucks.

questions.questionText: LeBron James, who broke the NBA's all-time scoring record in 2023, plays for which team as of 2024?
questions.tidbits: LeBron James has also played for the Cleveland Cavaliers and Miami Heat across his record four NBA championships.

questions.questionText: Which Serbian center won three NBA MVP awards (2021, 2022, 2024) in four years and led the Denver Nuggets to their first championship?
questions.tidbits: Nikola Jokic was drafted 41st overall in the 2014 draft — one of the greatest draft steals in NBA history.

questions.questionText: Which French center, standing 7'4", was selected first overall by the San Antonio Spurs in the 2023 NBA Draft?
questions.tidbits: Victor Wembanyama is considered a generational prospect whose wingspan, shot-blocking, and ball-handling are historically unprecedented.

questions.questionText: Which Slovenian guard plays for the Dallas Mavericks and is considered one of the best scorers and passers of his generation?
questions.tidbits: Luka Doncic was named Rookie of the Year in 2019 and became one of the youngest players ever to score 50+ points in a playoff game.

questions.questionText: Which Golden State Warriors guard coaches Steve Kerr has called the greatest shooter in NBA history, known for revolutionizing the three-point game?
questions.tidbits: Stephen Curry's shooting influence is so vast that the entire NBA shifted its offensive strategy toward three-point shooting in the 2010s.

questions.questionText: Which Memphis Grizzlies guard faced multiple gun-related suspensions in 2023 and 2024 that significantly derailed his career?
questions.tidbits: Ja Morant was suspended for 25 games in 2023-24 after repeatedly posting videos on social media displaying firearms.

questions.questionText: Which Miami Heat coach, known for his defensive system and player development, led his team to NBA Finals appearances in 2020 and 2023?
questions.tidbits: Erik Spoelstra has been the Heat's head coach since 2008 and won back-to-back championships with the Big Three in 2012 and 2013.

questions.questionText: Which Boston Celtics forward, selected with the fourth overall pick in 2021, won the 2022 Rookie of the Year award with the Toronto Raptors?
questions.tidbits: Scottie Barnes won Rookie of the Year despite being considered a lower-profile pick than 2021 first overall Cade Cunningham.

questions.questionText: Which Denver Nuggets head coach, who led them to the 2023 championship, previously coached the Sacramento Kings and is known for his offensive system?
questions.tidbits: Mike Malone became just the second Nuggets coach to win a championship, inheriting a team built patiently around Nikola Jokic.

questions.questionText: Which team won the 2023 NBA Championship — the first in their franchise history?
questions.tidbits: The Nuggets beat the Miami Heat 4-1 in the 2023 Finals, with Nikola Jokic earning Finals MVP.

questions.questionText: Which team won the 2024 NBA Championship, earning their record 18th title?
questions.tidbits: The Celtics beat the Dallas Mavericks 4-1 in the 2024 Finals. Jaylen Brown won Finals MVP.

questions.questionText: Which player won the 2021 NBA Finals MVP after scoring 50 points in Game 6 of the Finals?
questions.tidbits: Giannis's 50-point performance in Game 6 is one of the best Finals performances in NBA history. The Bucks beat the Suns 4-2.

questions.questionText: Which player won the 2022 NBA Finals MVP, his first ever Finals MVP, as the Golden State Warriors beat the Boston Celtics 4-2?
questions.tidbits: Stephen Curry had won three championships before 2022 but had never won Finals MVP — he averaged 31.2 points in the 2022 Finals.

questions.questionText: Which player won the 2020 NBA Finals MVP as the Los Angeles Lakers beat the Miami Heat 4-2 in the NBA Bubble?
questions.tidbits: LeBron's 2020 title was his fourth and first with the Lakers. He averaged 29.8 points, 11.8 rebounds, and 8.5 assists in the Finals.

questions.questionText: Who won the 2023 regular season NBA MVP, becoming the first player to win from a team with a losing record?
questions.tidbits: Joel Embiid's MVP was controversial because the Philadelphia 76ers struggled in the second half of the season, leading to debates about the award criteria.

questions.questionText: Victor Wembanyama won the 2024 Rookie of the Year award. Who won the 2023 Rookie of the Year?
questions.tidbits: Paolo Banchero, the first overall pick in 2022 by the Orlando Magic, became the first first-year player to win ROY after being drafted first overall since Blake Griffin in 2011.

questions.questionText: Nikola Jokic won three MVPs in four years (2021, 2022, 2024). Who won the MVP in the year between his awards — 2023?
questions.tidbits: Embiid won the 2023 MVP representing the Philadelphia 76ers — it was the first MVP of his career after multiple near-misses.

questions.questionText: Who was the number one overall pick in the 2021 NBA Draft?
questions.tidbits: Cade Cunningham was considered a franchise-changing player for the rebuilding Detroit Pistons, who had the worst record in the 2020-21 season.

questions.questionText: Which player won Finals MVP for the Celtics in 2024, making him the first Celtics player to win Finals MVP since Paul Pierce in 2008?
questions.tidbits: Jaylen Brown's 2024 Finals MVP sparked debate because many felt Jayson Tatum had a comparable series, but Brown was consistent and clutch throughout.

questions.questionText: The 2020 NBA season was completed inside a 'Bubble' to protect players from COVID-19. In which Florida city was the Bubble located?
questions.answerText: Orlando (at Disney World's ESPN Wide World of Sports complex)
questions.tidbits: The NBA Bubble ran from July to October 2020 with no fans, strict COVID protocols, and all teams housed at Disney World.

questions.questionText: On what date did LeBron James break Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's all-time NBA scoring record?
questions.tidbits: LeBron passed Kareem's record of 38,387 points during a game against the Oklahoma City Thunder in Los Angeles.

questions.questionText: Stephen Curry broke the all-time NBA three-point record in December 2021, surpassing which former record holder?
questions.tidbits: Curry passed Ray Allen's record of 2,973 made three-pointers on December 14, 2021, against the Knicks at Madison Square Garden.

questions.questionText: During the 2020 NBA Bubble, players and coaches wore social justice messages on their jerseys instead of names. What movement was prominently displayed?
questions.answerText: Black Lives Matter (players wore messages like 'Black Lives Matter,' 'Say Her Name,' 'I Can't Breathe')
questions.tidbits: The NBA became the first major North American sports league to prominently integrate social justice messaging into games during the 2020 season.

questions.questionText: Which NBA superstar announced in 2023 that his son Bronny James had been drafted by the Lakers, making them the first father-son duo to play simultaneously in the NBA?
questions.tidbits: Bronny James was selected 55th overall in the 2024 NBA Draft by the Los Angeles Lakers, fulfilling LeBron's longtime dream of playing with his son.

questions.questionText: Which documentary series, released in 2020, chronicled Michael Jordan and the Chicago Bulls' final championship season and became a global cultural phenomenon?
questions.tidbits: 'The Last Dance' was released during the 2020 COVID shutdown and became one of the most-watched sports documentaries of all time.

questions.questionText: Victor Wembanyama's extraordinary combination of height, wingspan, shooting, and shot-blocking led NBA analysts to call him what kind of historically rare prospect?
questions.answerText: A generational or unicorn prospect (a once-in-a-generation talent)
questions.tidbits: LeBron James himself called Wembanyama 'an alien' — saying there had never been anyone like him in the history of basketball.

questions.questionText: Which country does Nikola Jokic represent internationally, and what is his country's basketball federation called?
questions.tidbits: Jokic has led Serbia to multiple FIBA World Cup medals and is considered Serbia's greatest basketball player.

questions.questionText: The NBA's global appeal expanded significantly in the 2020s. Which country produced the most international players in the NBA as of the 2023-24 season?
questions.tidbits: Canada consistently produces the most NBA players outside the US, fueled by the growth of grassroots basketball programs and the Toronto Raptors' influence.

questions.questionText: Giannis Antetokounmpo signed a supermax extension with the Bucks in 2020 worth approximately how much — setting a record at the time?
questions.tidbits: Giannis's decision to commit to Milwaukee rather than test free agency was seen as a validation of the Bucks' franchise-building approach.

questions.questionText: How many points does a three-pointer count for in the NBA?
questions.tidbits: The NBA introduced the three-point line in 1979. Stephen Curry's era has seen three-point attempts more than double league-wide.

questions.questionText: What is a triple-double in basketball?
questions.answerText: Reaching double digits (10 or more) in three statistical categories in a single game — typically points, rebounds, and assists
questions.tidbits: Nikola Jokic holds the record for most career triple-doubles by a center and among the most all-time by any player.

questions.questionText: How many teams are in the NBA?
questions.tidbits: The NBA's 30 teams are split evenly between the Eastern Conference and Western Conference, 15 teams each.

questions.questionText: Nikola Jokic set new standards for big-man playmaking in the 2020s. What assists-per-game average did he post in the 2021-22 season, unprecedented for a center?
questions.tidbits: Jokic's 7.9 APG in 2021-22 was the highest ever for a player who also led the league in rebounding in the same season.

questions.questionText: LeBron James became the NBA's all-time leading scorer in 2023, surpassing what total points record set by Kareem Abdul-Jabbar?
questions.tidbits: Kareem's record had stood for 38 years since he broke Wilt Chamberlain's record in 1984.

questions.questionText: How many three-pointers has Stephen Curry made as of the 2024-25 season — approximately how far ahead of second place is he?
questions.answerText: Over 3,700 three-pointers (more than 700 ahead of second place Ray Allen's former record)
questions.tidbits: Curry passed Ray Allen's 2,973 record in 2021 and has continued to extend the record by hundreds of makes each season.

questions.questionText: Victor Wembanyama stands 7'4" with an extraordinary wingspan. What is his approximate wingspan in inches?
questions.tidbits: Wembanyama's wingspan of around 8 feet allows him to contest shots from distances that no other player in NBA history could reach.

questions.questionText: Nikola Jokic won the 2021 MVP by averaging 26.4 points, 10.8 rebounds, and 8.3 assists. What makes this statistical profile historically unique for a center?
questions.answerText: No center in NBA history had ever averaged those assist numbers while also leading in scoring and rebounding — it is a point guard stat line combined with a big man's physical dominance.
questions.tidbits: Jokic's combination of size and playmaking forced teams to reconsider traditional positional roles, pioneering the 'point center' archetype.

questions.questionText: What is the NBA's shot clock rule — how many seconds does a team have to attempt a shot after gaining possession?
questions.tidbits: The 24-second shot clock was introduced in 1954 by NBA president Maurice Podoloff after Danny Biasone calculated the ideal pace for an exciting game.

questions.questionText: Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 50 points in Game 6 of the 2021 Finals — setting a record for what specific Finals statistical achievement?
questions.answerText: Most points in a series-clinching Finals game by any player in NBA history
questions.tidbits: Giannis also added 14 rebounds and 5 blocks in the 50-point performance, one of the most complete Finals games ever played.

questions.questionText: In which unusual location did the 2020 NBA season conclude, allowing play to continue safely during the COVID-19 pandemic?
questions.answerText: The NBA Bubble at Disney World (ESPN Wide World of Sports), Orlando, Florida
questions.tidbits: 22 teams entered the Bubble in July 2020; the league completed the full playoffs without a single COVID-19 transmission inside the controlled environment.

questions.questionText: The 2021 NBA Finals pitted the Milwaukee Bucks against which team from the Western Conference?
questions.tidbits: The Suns had the best record in the league in 2020-21 but were defeated 4-2 by the Bucks, denying Chris Paul his first championship.

questions.questionText: How many games did the Celtics need to defeat the Mavericks in the 2024 NBA Finals?
questions.tidbits: Luka Doncic struggled in Games 4 and 5 while Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum carried the Celtics to the 18th championship.

questions.questionText: Which team did the Miami Heat stun in the first round of the 2023 playoffs, even though that team held the top seed in the Eastern Conference?
questions.tidbits: The eighth-seeded Heat swept the first-seed Bucks in the first round in one of the biggest upsets in recent NBA playoff history.

questions.questionText: In the 2022 NBA Finals, which team did the Golden State Warriors defeat to win their fourth championship in eight years?
questions.tidbits: The 2022 Finals featured two of the most storied franchises — Warriors vs. Celtics — meeting in the Finals for the first time since 1964.

questions.questionText: Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered a 50-point, 14-rebound, 5-block performance in which game of the 2021 NBA Finals?
questions.tidbits: The 50-point game came when the Bucks were up 3-2 and needed a win to clinch — Giannis delivered in historic fashion.

questions.questionText: The Denver Nuggets' 2023 championship run featured Nikola Jokic at historic levels. Which team did they sweep in the Western Conference Finals?
questions.tidbits: The Nuggets swept the Lakers 4-0 in the 2023 Western Conference Finals, handing LeBron James and the Lakers a decisive defeat.

questions.questionText: The Miami Heat made the 2023 NBA Finals as an eighth seed — the lowest seed ever to reach the Finals. Which top seed did they eliminate in the East?
questions.answerText: Milwaukee Bucks (1 seed), then New York Knicks (5 seed), then Boston Celtics (2 seed)
questions.tidbits: The Heat's 2023 run is one of the greatest Cinderella stories in NBA Finals history, though they ultimately fell to the Nuggets in five games.

questions.questionText: What record did the Golden State Warriors set during the 2022 playoff run that highlights Stephen Curry's Finals impact?
questions.answerText: Curry became the oldest Finals MVP at age 34 and the first player to win Finals MVP without winning regular-season MVP in the same year since 1999
questions.tidbits: Curry's 2022 Finals MVP silenced critics who questioned whether he could dominate without Kevin Durant.

questions.questionText: In the 2020 Western Conference Finals, which surprise team defeated the Los Angeles Clippers despite trailing 3-1 in the series, before losing to the Lakers in the conference finals?
questions.tidbits: The Nuggets' 2020 run was remarkable — they also trailed the Utah Jazz 3-1 in the first round and came back. Nikola Jokic's star power was first fully recognized nationally during these playoffs.
